title: lachemilla glandulosa
intro: Lachemilla glandulosa, also known as sticky wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Rosaceae, native to the Andes region in South America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Lachemilla glandulosa include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and treating skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate minor skin conditions.
